title: AST Bear Bait Violation: Anchorage [AK25048924]
summary: On 05/03/2025 Alaska Wildlife Troopers conducted a bear bait patrol in the Portage Valley. A bait registered to Kyle Todd was found to be less than a mile from a dwelling. On 05/22/2025 Todd was issued a mandatory court appearance citation for using black bear bait near a building.

title: AST Residency Investigation: Anchorage [AK25044764]
summary: On 04/30/2025 Andrey Dankanich was stopped for speeding along the Seward Highway. During the contact, fishing gear was observed in the vehicle and Dankanich admitted to fishing that day. Dankanich provided an out of state driver’s license and resident fishing license. Investigation later revealed Dankanich does not meet Alaska residency requirements. On 05/22/25 Dankanich was issued a citation for false statements on a license application.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: for false statements on a license application

title: AST DUI, Leaving the Scene: Fairbanks [AK25048861]
summary: On May 22, 2025, at approximately 1009 hours, the Alaska State Trooper responded to the Holmes Transfer Site for the report of a hit and run. The suspect vehicle was located in the Shannon Park Neighborhood of Fairbanks. Investigation revealed that Timothy Williams was under the influence of alcohol when he hit another vehicle and left the accident without providing information. Timothy was remanded to the Fairbanks Correctional Center.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Motorcycle collision: Kodiak [AK25047895]
summary: On May 19, 2025, Alaska State Troopers received a report just after 11 p.m. of a motorcycle collision on Rezanof Drive in Kodiak. The initial investigation indicates the motorcycle went off the road after it navigated a curve and struck a culvert at a high rate of speed. No other vehicles were invol...
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: New Sculpture Welcomes Visitors, Locals to Sugpiaq Lands and Waters
summary: KODIAK, Alaska — Koniag and Kodiak Area Native Association (KANA) dedicated a powerful new sculpture on Near Island Wednesday, welcoming visitors and locals to Kodiak and celebrating the region’s deep cultural heritage. Crafted with steel and aluminum by local artist Mark Witteveen, the sculpture is...

title: Petersburg Borough considers limiting sales tax exemption to low-income seniors
summary: Petersburg Borough officials say they are low on revenue these days. (Elizabeth Jenkins/KFSK) Listen to the story: Nearly a quarter of Petersburg’s residents are seniors, and the borough is losing out on sales tax, according to borough officials. That’s because the borough has a decades-old senior sales tax exemption. Now, the borough wants to limit that exemption to just low-income seniors to help make ends meet. For over fou...
